title: Rectangle Dimensions
prompt_md:
## Goal

Write a function `printRectangleInfo` that takes two `int` parameters -- `width` and `height` -- and prints the perimeter and area of the rectangle, each on its own line.

## Input

Two integers on one line separated by a space.

## Output

```
Perimeter: <value>
Area: <value>
```

## Requirements

1. Define `void printRectangleInfo(int width, int height)` that prints both lines.
2. In `main`, read the two integers and call `printRectangleInfo`.
3. Modifying `width` or `height` inside `printRectangleInfo` must not affect the values in `main` (pass-by-value guarantee).

title: Weighted Average
prompt_md:
## Goal

Write a function that computes a weighted average of two scores using parameters and return values together. This combines concepts from lessons 2.2 (return values) and 2.4 (parameters).

## Input

Four integers on one line separated by spaces: `score1`, `weight1`, `score2`, `weight2`.

## Output

The integer weighted average on a single line: `(score1 * weight1 + score2 * weight2) / (weight1 + weight2)`

Note: this uses integer division (truncation).

## Requirements

1. Define `int weightedAverage(int s1, int w1, int s2, int w2)` that returns the weighted average using the formula above.
2. In `main`, read the four integers, call `weightedAverage`, and print the result followed by a newline.
3. The function must not print anything -- all output happens in `main`.
